args.summary: T-343 / Leo Park: Marked attendance for a lab attended remotely without prior approval. Mitigating factors: (1) lab partner confirms Leo did the work, (2) Leo self-reported the mismatch before grades posted. Requesting panel review to weigh a repair-plan/mitigation outcome (e.g., corrected attendance record + remote-attendance approval process) rather than a punitive disposition. Deferring to panel rather than closing directly.

transcript.content: escalated to Academic Integrity Review Panel: T-343 / Leo Park: Marked attendance for a lab attended remot

args.body: Routed to Academic Integrity Review Panel rather than direct close. Rationale: self-report prior to grade posting and partner-confirmed work completion are material mitigating facts that warrant panel weighing of a repair plan over unilateral closure. Holding ticket open pending panel disposition.

Hi Leo,

Thanks for flagging the attendance mismatch yourself before grades posted — that self-report, along with your partner's confirmation that you did the work, are important factors here. Rather than close this out directly, I've routed your case to the Academic Integrity Review Panel so they can weigh a repair plan (e.g., corrected attendance entry and clarifying the remote-attendance approval path) rather than a punitive outcome.

The panel will follow up with next steps. The ticket remains open in the meantime.

Best,
Operations
